jokes.text: And we're out of business. Good news! We're back in business! We've been hired to deliver an envelope.
jokes.setup: Company goes out of business, seems like end of episode
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal with trivial delivery job
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor Farnsworth misdirection - his trademark 'Good news!' immediately following bad news. Perfect character integration and economy.

jokes.text: You're the finest crew I've had the honor to work with. Oh, my. We're being foreclosed upon. You're all terrible and incompetent.
jokes.setup: Sincere praise for the crew after successful delivery
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal to insults upon reading foreclosure notice
jokes.explanation: Another classic Professor flip-flop, though less surprising than the first. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Wait, I found a loophole in the mortgage. If we actually pay it, we can keep the building.
jokes.setup: Hope for a clever legal loophole to avoid foreclosure
jokes.punchline: The 'loophole' is just paying the mortgage normally
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of expectation - the audience expects a clever legal trick, gets basic responsibility instead. Perfect deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: while us men reminisce and smoke some stogies
jokes.setup: Division of labor during crisis - women do practical work
jokes.punchline: Men will do stereotypical 'manly' but useless activities
jokes.explanation: Decent gender stereotype joke but fairly predictable. Sets up the episode's gender theme.

jokes.text: We are not packing lunches, you walking beer commercials.
jokes.punchline: Leela's cutting insult about masculine stereotypes
jokes.explanation: 'Walking beer commercials' is a sharp, specific insult that perfectly captures toxic masculinity in an original phrase.

jokes.text: We're a delivery company!
jokes.setup: Fry defending free delivery as getting lots of customers
jokes.punchline: Oblivious to why a delivery company offering free delivery is problematic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - his stupidity is so complete he doesn't understand the basic economics of their business model.

jokes.text: Wow. That's actually a really good idea, for a woman.
jokes.punchline: Fry's backhanded compliment reveals his sexism
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe comedy - character says something inappropriate but it's predictable sexist humor.

jokes.text: I promised myself I wouldn't pose naked until I was married.
jokes.punchline: Amy's oddly specific and innocent moral standard
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Amy - her arbitrary moral line is both innocent and absurd.

jokes.text: Here's mine. 'All female employees must pose nude if requested.' Sounds fair.
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's contract has the same clause, he thinks it's reasonable
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist joke - Zoidberg having to pose nude as a male crustacean is perfectly illogical yet he accepts it.

jokes.text: Over. Time is money, peaches. Now shut your com-link and make love to the camera. And remember, I'm the camera.
jokes.punchline: His creepy photographer persona escalates to personal involvement
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from professional photographer talk to creepy personal involvement. Very in-character for Bender.

jokes.text: Whatever. I ran out of film an hour ago.
jokes.punchline: Reveals he hasn't been actually photographing anything
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - his laziness undermines his own scheme. Good misdirection and economy.

jokes.text: No, no, this won't do. A three-month calendar? What is this, Mercury?
jokes.punchline: Mercury reference - shortest orbit, shortest calendar
jokes.explanation: Clever astronomy joke that works on multiple levels - Mercury has shortest orbit and year, making a 3-month calendar logical.

jokes.text: But that was my idea! Then you should have talked louder. What?
jokes.setup: Professor claims the airline idea as his own
jokes.punchline: His dismissive response and confused 'What?' when she objects
jokes.explanation: Good callback to establish pattern, Professor's oblivious 'What?' adds character depth.

jokes.text: Sorry, ma'am, I'll have to confiscate your artificial kneecap. Okay, here you go.
jokes.punchline: Amy casually removes and hands over her kneecap
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - takes airport security absurdity to logical extreme. Amy's casual compliance makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Who ever heard of a plane with a woman President?
jokes.punchline: Fry's sexist dismissal using 'President' instead of 'pilot'
jokes.explanation: Standard sexism joke, though the word choice 'President' instead of 'pilot' adds slight originality.

jokes.text: Welcome to Plan Am flight one to Acapulsar. The local time on Acapulsar is five years from now.
jokes.punchline: Pan Am pun and time dilation from pulsar physics
jokes.explanation: Smart sci-fi comedy combining airline pun with real physics of time dilation near pulsars.

jokes.text: In the event of a wormhole sending us back in time, do not kill your parents. If you are traveling with small children, help them not to kill you before not killing your own parents.
jokes.punchline: Time travel paradox safety instructions with confusing logic
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist comedy - takes airline safety announcement format and applies it to sci-fi concepts with increasingly confusing logic.

jokes.text: So what's the deal with airline food? They offer you two choices, but they're always out of one. By the way, we're out of both choices.
jokes.punchline: Escalates the problem - they're out of both choices
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation of classic airline comedy trope. Takes familiar complaint and makes it worse.

jokes.text: Shut up and put on four episodes of The Office.
jokes.punchline: Passenger demands specific modern comfort
jokes.explanation: Weak pop culture reference joke without much craft or originality.

jokes.text: Are we at the fair yet, Mommy? We're not at the anyplace! And we're out of fuel!
jokes.setup: Child asking innocent question about arrival
jokes.punchline: Leela's frustrated response reveals they're completely lost
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Leela's competence being undermined. 'Anyplace' is a nice touch.

jokes.text: Please remain seated until the plane comes to a complete crash.
jokes.setup: Standard airline announcement about remaining seated
jokes.punchline: Subverts expectation by announcing crash instead of stop
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke with perfect economy. Subverts familiar airline announcement format perfectly.

jokes.text: Roller Derby is not a society.
jokes.punchline: Hermes's dismissive but accurate observation
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response that reveals character backgrounds while being logically sound.

jokes.text: We can't trust a woman's math. Count again.
jokes.punchline: Bender's sexist dismissal of basic counting
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe sexism joke, in character for Bender but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Well, you sure can run something, your mouth!
jokes.setup: LaBarbara saying he couldn't run a bath
jokes.punchline: Classic comeback format with marital tension
jokes.explanation: Very standard insult comeback with no originality or craft. Predictable marital bickering.

jokes.text: Yeahs. You knows, guyses, galses, in that order. Ows!
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's garbled explanation with obvious bias and immediate karma
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Zoidberg's speech pattern combined with immediate physical punishment for sexism.

jokes.text: My species has only a single gender, known as 'neuchacho.'
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau of 'neutral' and 'muchacho'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining neutral gender concept with Spanish casual address. Original construction.

jokes.text: Chocolate, Mac, men. The end.
jokes.setup: Rock alien's examples of things where one is better
jokes.punchline: Bender's immediate biased conclusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - jumps to conclusion without thinking. Good economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Which is larger, an Italian size four or an American Apparel medium? How many calories are in a small green tea yogurt? Name any 12 of the Desperate Housewives. How was your day?
jokes.setup: Switching to questions instead of drinking contests
jokes.punchline: Increasingly stereotypical 'women's knowledge' questions ending with relationship question
jokes.explanation: Good escalation through gender stereotypes, though the premise is fairly predictable. The final question is the best.

jokes.text: That was a test to see who could lie better. The men win that round. In your face, decumbent urinators.
jokes.setup: Apparent unfair scoring of gender test
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was testing dishonesty, plus elaborate insult
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and 'decumbent urinators' is a wonderfully pretentious way to say 'sitting down to pee.'

jokes.text: In your face, decumbent urinators.
jokes.punchline: Overly fancy way to say women sit to pee
jokes.explanation: 'Decumbent urinators' is hilariously pretentious language for a crude concept - very quotable

jokes.text: You will all be boiled alive, like retired circus animals,
jokes.setup: Warning about planet getting too hot
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and dark comparison to circus animals
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - the specificity of 'retired circus animals' is both unexpected and disturbing.

jokes.text: And may the inferior gender burst into something. Flames, I guess.
jokes.punchline: Trails off uncertainly about what they should burst into
jokes.explanation: Good anticlimax - starts dramatically then becomes uncertain and casual. Good economy.

jokes.text: Howdy there. You fellas need some directions? No, we're fine. Don't worry about it.
jokes.punchline: Fry's masculine pride refuses help despite being obviously lost
jokes.explanation: Classic male behavior joke - refusing to ask for directions. Well-executed but predictable.

jokes.text: A clearance sale! What? Where?
jokes.punchline: Distracted by mirage of shopping opportunity
jokes.explanation: Standard gender stereotype joke about women and shopping, but executed competently.

jokes.text: It wasn't Tommy Hilfijigger. It was Linens 'n Things! The hell it was. It was a Juicy Couture.
jokes.setup: Discussion of what the shopping mirage was
jokes.punchline: Each woman saw different store, Amy mispronounces Tommy Hilfiger
jokes.explanation: Good character work - each sees their preferred store. Amy's mispronunciation is classic character comedy.

jokes.text: So if anyone's got any good fart jokes, now would be the time.
jokes.setup: Facing death, time for last words
jokes.punchline: Fry's priority is toilet humor
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - even facing death, Fry wants lowbrow humor. Meta commentary on comedy itself.

jokes.text: Wait, I forgot that Amana here is a refrigerator.
jokes.punchline: Casual reminder that their friend is literally an appliance
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist moment - treats artificial character like normal person then remembers their function.

jokes.text: Was that a fart joke? Because I don't find those amusing. No one does.
jokes.punchline: Bender's pretentious dismissal of fart jokes despite being in one
jokes.explanation: Good callback with character irony - Bender dismisses fart jokes while being central to one.

jokes.text: How do you live with yourself? Damn it, woman, you're making me highly Selassie! Don't you talk to me that way, you big, hairy Belafonte.
jokes.punchline: Using Jamaican cultural figures as nonsensical insults
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific humor - only Hermes would use Haile Selassie and Harry Belafonte as nonsensical curse words. Very quotable and original.

jokes.text: That's not back bacon! That's your back baking!
jokes.setup: LaBarbara thanks Hermes for cooking her back bacon
jokes.punchline: His back is literally cooking in the heat
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay combining food term with literal situation. Good economy and structure.

jokes.text: Never bet against me being stupid.
jokes.setup: Rock alien lost bet about humans working together
jokes.punchline: Fry's proud declaration of his own stupidity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry line - takes pride in his own stupidity. Excellent character integration and quotable.

jokes.text: My wingwang's gone! My girls! My antenna! My kajigger! My gonopores! Look it up.
jokes.setup: Characters discovering their genders have been removed
jokes.punchline: Increasingly creative euphemisms ending with scientific term and instruction
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of euphemisms from silly to scientific. 'Look it up' adds educational comedy element.

jokes.text: Work goes fasters withouts no secondary sex characteristicses at which to hoot.
jokes.setup: Explaining why work is going well
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's garbled explanation of reduced sexual distraction
jokes.explanation: Good character work with Zoidberg's speech pattern making academic observation crude.

jokes.text: A neutopia.
jokes.punchline: Fry's proud portmanteau coining 'neutopia'
jokes.explanation: Clever portmanteau - neuter + utopia. Fry being proud of wordplay is endearing and in character

jokes.text: A neutopia.
jokes.setup: Describing the place as neutered utopia
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau combining neutral/neutered with utopia
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's also the episode title. Excellent economy and quotable.

jokes.text: It's about loving, and fighting, and that Rasta McNasty we were doing last night.
jokes.setup: Explaining what being human means beyond happiness
jokes.punchline: Crude euphemism for sex using fake Jamaican name
jokes.explanation: Good character moment where Fry gets philosophical then immediately crude. 'Rasta McNasty' is creative.

jokes.text: Now I got a whatchacallit instead of a kajigger, you stupid whatchacallit!
jokes.punchline: Bender's confusion with euphemisms, uses new anatomy as insult
jokes.explanation: Good callback to established euphemism, Bender's confusion and anger are in character.

jokes.text: I gotta get up five times a night to play Xbox.
jokes.setup: Hermes explaining why he needs sleep as a woman
jokes.punchline: Reveals stereotypically male behavior continues
jokes.explanation: Good observation about gaming habits. Shows personality transcending physical form.

jokes.text: For example, did you know there are more than two feelings?
jokes.punchline: Revelation about emotional complexity as discovery
jokes.explanation: Excellent gender commentary presented as genuine discovery. Great economy and insight.

jokes.text: These bad boys are hard to aim.
jokes.setup: Amy as male cleaning the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to urination difficulties
jokes.explanation: Basic bathroom humor with gender swap. Competent but not particularly original.

jokes.text: OMG! Being chicks is so much fun! Now when I say stupid things, guys all laugh and buy me stuff.
jokes.setup: Women supposedly having harder time adjusting
jokes.punchline: Fry discovers gender privilege benefits
jokes.explanation: Good social commentary through character discovery. Fry's oblivious joy at exploitation is in character.

jokes.text: Thank God most of our fans are huge perverts.
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging the fan base's motivations directly
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta humor - acknowledges the show's own fan service directly and bluntly.

jokes.text: I don't care what parts I have, as long as they interlock with yours.
jokes.punchline: Romantic declaration using mechanical metaphor
jokes.explanation: Excellent romantic comedy line using mechanical metaphor. Perfect for Hermes' character and very quotable.

jokes.text: It combines the contentedness of being neutered, with the occasional sex of being not.
jokes.punchline: Clinical description of marital sexual frequency
jokes.explanation: Brilliant marriage joke with perfect economy and structure. Clinical tone makes intimate subject matter funnier. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: You folks watch your genitals now.
jokes.punchline: Parting advice about protecting anatomy
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan farewell that subverts Western movie convention with anatomical advice. Good economy.
